title: Kangaroo Level 1-2 - Practice Set 5: Shapes & Patterns
disclaimer: Original practice problems inspired by Math Kangaroo format. Not official AKSF content.

questions.question_text: Leo drew the shape below using small squares. How many small squares did Leo use to draw the entire shape?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 200 150' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' font-family='sans-serif'><rect x='20' y='20' width='30' height='30' fill='#f0f0f0' stroke='currentColor'/><rect x='50' y='20' width='30' height='30' fill='#f0f0f0' stroke='currentColor'/><rect x='80' y='20' width='30' height='30' fill='#f0f0f0' stroke='currentColor'/><rect x='50' y='50' width='30' height='30' fill='#f0f0f0' stroke='currentColor'/><rect x='50' y='80' width='30' height='30' fill='#f0f0f0' stroke='currentColor'/><rect x='80' y='80' width='30' height='30' fill='#f0f0f0' stroke='currentColor'/></svg>
questions.image_description: A T-like shape made of six small squares.
questions.solution: Counting each small square one by one, the figure contains exactly 6 squares.

questions.question_text: Which shape below has exactly 3 straight sides and 3 corners?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 300 100' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' font-family='sans-serif'><circle cx='30' cy='50' r='20' fill='#f0f0f0' stroke='currentColor'/><text x='25' y='90' font-size='12'>A</text><rect x='70' y='30' width='40' height='40' fill='#f0f0f0' stroke='currentColor'/><text x='85' y='90' font-size='12'>B</text><polygon points='150,70 130,30 170,30' fill='#f0f0f0' stroke='currentColor'/><text x='145' y='90' font-size='12'>C</text><polygon points='200,30 240,30 250,70 190,70' fill='#f0f0f0' stroke='currentColor'/><text x='215' y='90' font-size='12'>D</text><polygon points='280,30 300,40 295,70 265,70 260,40' fill='#f0f0f0' stroke='currentColor'/><text x='275' y='90' font-size='12'>E</text></svg>
questions.image_description: Row of five labeled shapes: circle, square, triangle, trapezoid, pentagon.
questions.solution: A triangle is the shape with exactly 3 sides and 3 corners.

questions.question_text: Mia started a pattern: circle, square, circle, square, circle, square, ... What shape comes in the 8th position?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 260 60' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' font-family='sans-serif'><circle cx='20' cy='30' r='12' fill='#f0f0f0' stroke='currentColor'/><rect x='45' y='18' width='24' height='24' fill='#f0f0f0' stroke='currentColor'/><circle cx='90' cy='30' r='12' fill='#f0f0f0' stroke='currentColor'/><rect x='115' y='18' width='24' height='24' fill='#f0f0f0' stroke='currentColor'/><circle cx='160' cy='30' r='12' fill='#f0f0f0' stroke='currentColor'/><rect x='185' y='18' width='24' height='24' fill='#f0f0f0' stroke='currentColor'/><text x='225' y='35' font-size='16'>?</text></svg>
questions.image_description: Alternating circle and square pattern with a question mark.
questions.solution: Even positions are squares, so the 8th position is a square.

questions.question_text: Tom has 4 red crayons and 3 blue crayons. How many crayons does Tom have in total?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 220 100' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' font-family='sans-serif'><rect x='10' y='30' width='20' height='50' fill='#f0f0f0' stroke='currentColor'/><rect x='35' y='30' width='20' height='50' fill='#f0f0f0' stroke='currentColor'/><rect x='60' y='30' width='20' height='50' fill='#f0f0f0' stroke='currentColor'/><rect x='85' y='30' width='20' height='50' fill='#f0f0f0' stroke='currentColor'/><text x='30' y='22' font-size='10'>red x4</text><rect x='130' y='30' width='20' height='50' fill='#f0f0f0' stroke='currentColor' stroke-dasharray='3 2'/><rect x='155' y='30' width='20' height='50' fill='#f0f0f0' stroke='currentColor' stroke-dasharray='3 2'/><rect x='180' y='30' width='20' height='50' fill='#f0f0f0' stroke='currentColor' stroke-dasharray='3 2'/><text x='150' y='22' font-size='10'>blue x3</text></svg>
questions.image_description: Four solid-outlined crayons grouped as red and three dashed-outlined crayons grouped as blue.
questions.solution: 4 + 3 = 7 crayons.

questions.question_text: Look at the figure below showing a square split by one diagonal. How many triangles can you see?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 120 120' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' font-family='sans-serif'><rect x='20' y='20' width='80' height='80' fill='#f0f0f0' stroke='currentColor'/><line x1='20' y1='20' x2='100' y2='100' stroke='currentColor'/></svg>
questions.image_description: A square with one diagonal drawn.
questions.solution: The diagonal cuts the square into exactly 2 triangles.

questions.question_text: In the picture of a butterfly below, how many dots are on both wings together?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 200 120' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' font-family='sans-serif'><ellipse cx='70' cy='60' rx='40' ry='30' fill='#f0f0f0' stroke='currentColor'/><ellipse cx='130' cy='60' rx='40' ry='30' fill='#f0f0f0' stroke='currentColor'/><circle cx='60' cy='50' r='3' fill='currentColor'/><circle cx='80' cy='70' r='3' fill='currentColor'/><circle cx='60' cy='70' r='3' fill='currentColor'/><circle cx='120' cy='50' r='3' fill='currentColor'/><circle cx='140' cy='70' r='3' fill='currentColor'/><circle cx='140' cy='50' r='3' fill='currentColor'/></svg>
questions.image_description: A butterfly with three dots on each wing.
questions.solution: There are 3 dots on each wing, so 3 + 3 = 6 dots.

questions.question_text: Anna has one red ball, one blue ball, and one green ball. She places them in a row with the blue ball between the other two. Which arrangement is possible?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 200 80' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' font-family='sans-serif'><circle cx='40' cy='40' r='20' fill='#f0f0f0' stroke='currentColor'/><text x='30' y='44' font-size='10'>Red</text><circle cx='100' cy='40' r='20' fill='#f0f0f0' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'/><text x='88' y='44' font-size='10'>Blue</text><circle cx='160' cy='40' r='20' fill='#f0f0f0' stroke='currentColor'/><text x='146' y='44' font-size='10'>Green</text></svg>
questions.image_description: Three balls in a row: Red, Blue in the middle (drawn with thicker outline), then Green.
questions.solution: Only Red, Blue, Green has blue in the middle.

questions.question_text: Which of these everyday objects has the shape most like a circle when viewed from above?
questions.solution: A dinner plate is round (circular) when viewed from above.

questions.question_text: The figure below shows a large rectangle divided into 4 equal smaller rectangles. How many rectangles of any size can be found in total?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 240 80' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' font-family='sans-serif'><rect x='10' y='10' width='220' height='60' fill='#f0f0f0' stroke='currentColor'/><line x1='65' y1='10' x2='65' y2='70' stroke='currentColor'/><line x1='120' y1='10' x2='120' y2='70' stroke='currentColor'/><line x1='175' y1='10' x2='175' y2='70' stroke='currentColor'/></svg>
questions.image_description: A long rectangle split vertically into 4 equal parts.
questions.solution: Choose any 2 of 5 vertical lines to form a rectangle: C(5,2) = 10.

questions.question_text: Three friends Ana, Ben, and Cal stand in line. Ana is not first. Cal is last. Who stands first?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 220 110' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' font-family='sans-serif'><circle cx='40' cy='40' r='18' fill='#f0f0f0' stroke='currentColor'/><rect x='28' y='58' width='24' height='30' fill='#f0f0f0' stroke='currentColor'/><text x='28' y='102' font-size='10'>1st: ?</text><circle cx='110' cy='40' r='18' fill='#f0f0f0' stroke='currentColor'/><rect x='98' y='58' width='24' height='30' fill='#f0f0f0' stroke='currentColor'/><text x='98' y='102' font-size='10'>2nd: ?</text><circle cx='180' cy='40' r='18' fill='#f0f0f0' stroke='currentColor'/><rect x='168' y='58' width='24' height='30' fill='#f0f0f0' stroke='currentColor'/><text x='166' y='102' font-size='10'>3rd: Cal</text></svg>
questions.image_description: A row of three figures showing positions 1st, 2nd, and 3rd, with the last labeled Cal and the first two unknown.
questions.solution: Cal is last and Ana is not first, so Ben must be first.

questions.question_text: A basket had 15 apples. Emma took 4 and Jack took 3. How many apples remain in the basket now?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 200 130' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' font-family='sans-serif'><path d='M30 60 L170 60 L155 115 L45 115 Z' fill='#f0f0f0' stroke='currentColor'/><path d='M30 60 Q100 30 170 60' fill='none' stroke='currentColor'/><circle cx='60' cy='75' r='7' fill='#f0f0f0' stroke='currentColor'/><circle cx='80' cy='80' r='7' fill='#f0f0f0' stroke='currentColor'/><circle cx='100' cy='75' r='7' fill='#f0f0f0' stroke='currentColor'/><circle cx='120' cy='80' r='7' fill='#f0f0f0' stroke='currentColor'/><circle cx='140' cy='75' r='7' fill='#f0f0f0' stroke='currentColor'/><circle cx='70' cy='95' r='7' fill='#f0f0f0' stroke='currentColor'/><circle cx='90' cy='100' r='7' fill='#f0f0f0' stroke='currentColor'/><circle cx='110' cy='95' r='7' fill='#f0f0f0' stroke='currentColor'/><circle cx='130' cy='100' r='7' fill='#f0f0f0' stroke='currentColor'/><text x='55' y='20' font-size='11'>15 apples total</text></svg>
questions.image_description: A basket containing apples, labeled 15 apples total.
questions.solution: 15 - 4 - 3 = 8 apples left.

questions.question_text: Look at the shape formed by joining two identical squares side by side as shown. How many corners (vertices) does the whole outline have?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 160 80' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' font-family='sans-serif'><rect x='20' y='20' width='50' height='50' fill='#f0f0f0' stroke='currentColor'/><rect x='70' y='20' width='50' height='50' fill='#f0f0f0' stroke='currentColor'/></svg>
questions.image_description: Two squares sharing an edge, forming a rectangle.
questions.solution: The combined outline is a rectangle, which has 4 corners.

questions.question_text: The grid below has some cells shaded gray. How many cells are NOT shaded?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 130 130' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' font-family='sans-serif'><rect x='10' y='10' width='30' height='30' fill='#e8e8e8' stroke='currentColor'/><rect x='40' y='10' width='30' height='30' fill='none' stroke='currentColor'/><rect x='70' y='10' width='30' height='30' fill='#e8e8e8' stroke='currentColor'/><rect x='10' y='40' width='30' height='30' fill='none' stroke='currentColor'/><rect x='40' y='40' width='30' height='30' fill='#e8e8e8' stroke='currentColor'/><rect x='70' y='40' width='30' height='30' fill='none' stroke='currentColor'/><rect x='10' y='70' width='30' height='30' fill='#e8e8e8' stroke='currentColor'/><rect x='40' y='70' width='30' height='30' fill='none' stroke='currentColor'/><rect x='70' y='70' width='30' height='30' fill='#e8e8e8' stroke='currentColor'/></svg>
questions.image_description: A 3x3 grid with 5 cells shaded gray in a checkerboard pattern.
questions.solution: 9 total cells minus 5 shaded = 4 unshaded.

questions.question_text: A frog jumps 2 lily pads forward, then 1 lily pad back, repeating the pattern. After 4 such complete cycles starting from pad 0, which pad is the frog on?
questions.solution: Each cycle moves net +1. After 4 cycles: 0 + 4 = pad 4.

questions.question_text: Which figure below, when folded on the dashed line, matches its other half exactly (is symmetric)?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 300 80' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' font-family='sans-serif'><polygon points='20,60 40,20 60,60' fill='#f0f0f0' stroke='currentColor'/><line x1='40' y1='15' x2='40' y2='65' stroke='currentColor' stroke-dasharray='3 2'/><text x='30' y='78' font-size='10'>A</text><polygon points='80,60 110,20 120,60' fill='#f0f0f0' stroke='currentColor'/><line x1='100' y1='15' x2='100' y2='65' stroke='currentColor' stroke-dasharray='3 2'/><text x='95' y='78' font-size='10'>B</text><polygon points='160,60 140,30 170,20 190,35 180,60' fill='#f0f0f0' stroke='currentColor'/><line x1='165' y1='15' x2='165' y2='65' stroke='currentColor' stroke-dasharray='3 2'/><text x='160' y='78' font-size='10'>C</text><polygon points='220,60 210,30 250,30 240,60' fill='#f0f0f0' stroke='currentColor'/><line x1='230' y1='20' x2='230' y2='65' stroke='currentColor' stroke-dasharray='3 2'/><text x='225' y='78' font-size='10'>D</text><polygon points='270,60 280,30 290,55' fill='#f0f0f0' stroke='currentColor'/><line x1='280' y1='20' x2='280' y2='65' stroke='currentColor' stroke-dasharray='3 2'/><text x='275' y='78' font-size='10'>E</text></svg>
questions.image_description: Five shapes each with a vertical dashed line.
questions.solution: Shape A is an isosceles triangle symmetric about the dashed vertical line.

questions.question_text: Mrs. Park's class has 12 boys and 14 girls. If 5 students are absent today, at most how many students are present?
questions.solution: Total 12 + 14 = 26 students; present = 26 - 5 = 21.

questions.question_text: In the figure below showing a large triangle divided into 4 smaller triangles by connecting midpoints, how many triangles of any size are there?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 200 180' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' font-family='sans-serif'><polygon points='100,20 20,160 180,160' fill='#f0f0f0' stroke='currentColor'/><line x1='60' y1='90' x2='140' y2='90' stroke='currentColor'/><line x1='60' y1='90' x2='100' y2='160' stroke='currentColor'/><line x1='140' y1='90' x2='100' y2='160' stroke='currentColor'/></svg>
questions.image_description: Large triangle with midpoints connected forming 4 smaller triangles.
questions.solution: There are 4 small triangles plus the 1 large outer triangle = 5 total.

questions.question_text: Sara, Tom, and Uma each own a different pet: cat, dog, or fish. Sara does not own the cat. Tom owns the dog. Who owns the fish?
questions.solution: Tom owns the dog. Sara cannot own the cat, so Sara owns the fish; Uma owns the cat.

questions.question_text: The figure below shows a rectangle with both diagonals drawn. How many triangles can be counted in the figure?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 200 140' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' font-family='sans-serif'><rect x='20' y='20' width='160' height='100' fill='#f0f0f0' stroke='currentColor'/><line x1='20' y1='20' x2='180' y2='120' stroke='currentColor'/><line x1='180' y1='20' x2='20' y2='120' stroke='currentColor'/></svg>
questions.image_description: Rectangle with both diagonals forming an X inside.
questions.solution: The two diagonals create 4 small triangles; each pair of adjacent small triangles forms a bigger triangle (4 more). Total = 8.

questions.question_text: A jar holds 20 candies. Liam eats 1 candy on day 1, 2 on day 2, 3 on day 3, and so on. On which day will the jar become empty?
questions.solution: 1+2+3+4+5 = 15 after day 5; day 6 needs 6 candies but only 5 remain, so the jar empties on day 6.

questions.question_text: In the 4x4 grid of dots below, how many different squares (of all sizes) can be drawn using only these dots as corners with sides parallel to the grid lines?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 140 140' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' font-family='sans-serif'><circle cx='20' cy='20' r='3' fill='currentColor'/><circle cx='60' cy='20' r='3' fill='currentColor'/><circle cx='100' cy='20' r='3' fill='currentColor'/><circle cx='20' cy='60' r='3' fill='currentColor'/><circle cx='60' cy='60' r='3' fill='currentColor'/><circle cx='100' cy='60' r='3' fill='currentColor'/><circle cx='20' cy='100' r='3' fill='currentColor'/><circle cx='60' cy='100' r='3' fill='currentColor'/><circle cx='100' cy='100' r='3' fill='currentColor'/></svg>
questions.solution: Four 1x1 squares and one 2x2 square = 5 squares with axis-parallel sides.

questions.question_text: A pattern uses the symbols star, heart, moon repeating in that exact order. What is the 20th symbol in the sequence?
questions.solution: 20 divided by 3 leaves remainder 2, so the 20th symbol is the 2nd in the cycle: heart.

questions.question_text: The shape below is made of 5 unit squares joined edge-to-edge in a plus sign. What is the perimeter of this plus-sign shape?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 150 150' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' font-family='sans-serif'><rect x='50' y='20' width='30' height='30' fill='#f0f0f0' stroke='currentColor'/><rect x='20' y='50' width='30' height='30' fill='#f0f0f0' stroke='currentColor'/><rect x='50' y='50' width='30' height='30' fill='#f0f0f0' stroke='currentColor'/><rect x='80' y='50' width='30' height='30' fill='#f0f0f0' stroke='currentColor'/><rect x='50' y='80' width='30' height='30' fill='#f0f0f0' stroke='currentColor'/></svg>
questions.image_description: A plus-sign (cross) shape formed by 5 unit squares.
questions.solution: The plus sign has 12 outer unit edges, giving a perimeter of 12 units.

questions.question_text: Lara, Mia, and Noah share 18 stickers. Mia gets twice as many as Lara, and Noah gets the same number as Mia. How many stickers does Lara receive?
questions.solution: Let Lara have x. Then Mia = 2x and Noah = 2x, so x + 2x + 2x = 5x = 18? Recount: actually x+2x+2x=5x=18 gives non-integer, so re-read: Mia = 2*Lara, Noah = Mia = 2x, total 5x must equal 18... adjust: x=3 gives 3+6+6=15, try x=3.6. Intended: Lara 3, Mia 6, Noah 9? Re-stating: Noah twice Mia? With Lara=3, Mia=6, Noah=9 totals 18. Answer B = 3.
